Resolves #10786

Authored by GPT-5.5 (Codex Desktop). Session 2821a9d4-7634-4fe7-a8a2-daf49253b929.

Serializes `harnessLifecycle.spec.mjs` at the file describe level because its tests intentionally share one `@neo-test-harness-agent` harness-state identity and therefore one state-file path. This preserves the shared-identity coverage while preventing default-worker Playwright runs from racing on disk.

Evidence: L2 (default-worker Playwright unit reproduction + passing targeted wake-substrate suite) -> L2 required (AC1-AC3 unit/spec validation). No residuals.

## Deltas from ticket
Implemented Option A, the smallest local guardrail: `test.describe.configure({mode: "serial"})` plus an inline comment explaining the shared state-file constraint. No production substrate changes.

## Test Evidence
- Before fix: `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/harnessLifecycle.spec.mjs` failed with 6 tests using 6 workers; `JSON.parse` read a concurrently-mutated shared state file.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/harnessLifecycle.spec.mjs` passed: 6 passed.
- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/harnessLifecycle.spec.mjs --workers=1` passed: 6 passed.
- Sandbox sibling batch hit expected write ceiling: readonly Memory Core SQLite and EPERM under `.neo-ai-data`.
- Escalated sibling batch passed: `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/checkSunsetted.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/resumeHarness.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/wakeSafetyGate.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/swarm-heartbeat.spec.mjs` -> 44 passed, 2 skipped.
- Escalated combined acceptance suite passed: same sibling set plus `harnessLifecycle.spec.mjs` -> 50 passed, 2 skipped.

## Post-Merge Validation
- [ ] None; the close-target ACs are fully covered by the targeted unit runs above.

## Commit
- `924985a9e` — `fix(test): serialize harness lifecycle spec (#10786)`

The 3-line `test.describe.configure({mode: 'serial'})` is the documented Playwright primitive for this exact failure mode (shared file-write across specs in same worker under fullyParallel). Approve+Follow-Up would be over-shaped — there are no non-blocking gaps that warrant a separate ticket; the tests pass and the comment captures the rationale.
> 
> **Peer-Review Opening:** Clean diagnosis + minimal-shape fix. The diff is exactly what the symptom called for, and the inline comment will save the next reader from re-deriving why the describe is serial.

> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge OR documented search (per guide §7.1):**
> 
> - **Follow-up concern (non-blocking):** the fix correctly serializes the existing 6 test cases that share `@neo-test-harness-agent`, but the underlying root cause is *coupling-to-shared-real-identity*. A future test that adds a 7th case using a parametrized identity would silently break the serial assumption (because the comment ties the rationale to "shared identity"). Architecturally cleaner long-term: per-test temp-path identity sanitization (each test case derives its own `@neo-test-temp-{uuid}` so writes never collide). Out-of-scope-for-#10786; would be a sibling-fileable polish ticket if anyone touches this spec again. Not blocking — the serial-config solves the symptom and is empirically validated.
>

> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: Playwright's `test.describe.configure({mode: 'serial'})` is the documented primitive for cross-spec-shared-file-write coupling under default-worker fullyParallel. When the test setup intentionally shares state (which preserves a real-world coverage shape — not all serialization is a code smell), serial-config inside the describe is the right tool over `--workers=1` global flag.

> ### 📊 Evaluation Metrics
> 
> *   **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 100 — `test.describe.configure({mode: 'serial'})` is the canonical Playwright primitive for this case; I actively considered (1) whether `--workers=1` global flag would have been simpler (no — it pessimizes the entire suite, not just this spec), (2) whether per-test cleanup could have replaced serialization (no — the writes during execution overlap, not just teardown), (3) whether a `test.beforeEach` reset with mutex would be cleaner (no — that's the heavier hammer; serial is the right gauge for shared-identity-by-design tests). None apply.
> *   **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 90 — 10 deducted because the comment captures the *constraint* but doesn't anchor *why* the shared identity exists (intentional shared-state coverage, not test-author error). > *   **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 100 — minimal change, correct primitive, tests pass first run independently verified. I actively considered: (1) whether `mode: 'serial'` inside an existing describe propagates to nested describes (no nested describes here, so non-issue), (2) whether the placement (line 24, after open-brace) was correct (it is — Playwright requires it inside the describe scope), (3) whether the test order had any sequencing dependencies that serialization introduces (audit of all 6 tests confirms none — each beforeEach/afterEach already isolates). None apply.
